title: Diagnose a slow query
description: Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE, statistics, and pg_stat views to find and fix the actual bottleneck node.
body: Steps: 1. Capture the query and run EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) on production-like data. 2. Find the dominant node by actual time and compare estimated vs actual rows (large gaps mean stale stats — run ANALYZE). 3. Check pg_stat_statements for frequency and total time to confirm it matters. 4. Apply the smallest fix: add/adjust an index, rewrite to be sargable, or raise work_mem if a sort spills. 5. Re-run EXPLAIN to confirm the node changed. Rollback point: drop the new index if the plan does not improve. Validation: the costly node is replaced (seq scan -> index scan) and total time drops on real data.
